videos.title: Christian vang Eghoff presenting the City Resilience Program
videos.description:
ABOUT THE CITY RESILIENCE PROGRAM
The City Resilience Program embeds resilience into urban planning, infrastructure investments, and government. The program supports cities to better understand and address their disasters and climate risks, and work with them to identify viable urban resilience inve

videos.title: Business continuity planning for SMEs: How to prepare for disasters | Training | UNDRR
videos.description:
🌪️ What happens to your business when a disaster strikes?
A flood. A heatwave. A power or internet outage. For small and medium enterprises, disruption can quickly become a crisis.

This training video explains business continuity planning (BCP)—and how it helps small and medium enterprises (SMEs) k
videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: What is a heat dome? | UNDRR
videos.description:
What exactly is a heat dome?
It’s when a high-pressure air system parks over a region, acting like a lid and locking in heat. When the jet stream stalls, extreme events like heatwaves, wildfires, or torrential rain can stay in place for days—or weeks.

💥 Climate change is making these stuck patterns
videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: How we stop tsunami disasters | UNDRR
videos.description:
When the sea suddenly surges or retreats, a tsunami could be coming 🌊.
While we can’t stop tsunamis, we can stop them from becoming disasters.

This video explains how countries and communities can prepare — and save lives — through:
✅ Early warning systems — because every minute counts
✅ Community
videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: We are 1 million voices strong | UNDRR
videos.description:
UNDRR now has one million followers on LinkedIn. 
 
This is more than a milestone – it is strong evidence of a growing global community that recognizes that understanding and reducing disaster risk saves lives.

videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: How one Amazon school is adapting to climate change | UNDRR
videos.description:
In the Brazilian Amazon, one small riverside school is proving that climate adaptation doesn’t need to be complex to be powerful. 🌿🏫

For three years, this community school has taken on heatwaves, floods, and dangerous riverbank erosion — and kept children safe through smart, locally led solutions:
videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: Why GDP isn’t ideal for measuring disaster costs | UNDRR
videos.description:
📉 GDP often gives a false picture after disasters. Economies may rebound quickly on paper during reconstruction, but behind the numbers, long-term social, environmental, and financial losses persist.

videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: This simple green trick can cool homes by 5°C | UNDRR
videos.description:
Hanging wall gardens could be a simple, green fix for rising urban heat 🌱🌡️

In many informal settlements, homes are poorly insulated and lack air conditioning — leaving residents with little escape from extreme temperatures. As cities heat up, nature-based solutions are proving their value.

videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.description:
What gets tracked gets funded.

Through a disaster and climate budget tagging exercise, gaps in spending were exposed and n
videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.description:
💡 What are Resilience Bonds—and why are they gaining momentum?

videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: There is no such thing as a natural disaster | UNDRR
videos.description:
A hazard can only become a disaster once it impacts on society or community. A hazard is natural, disasters are not.
💡 https://www.undrr.org/our-impact/campaigns/no-natural-disasters
videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.title: What are disaster funds and why do they matter? | UNDRR
videos.description:
💰 What are disaster funds—and why are they so important?

Disaster funds are money set aside by governments before a disaster strikes. They allow countries to act quickly, protect essential services, and begin recovery without delay.

videos.channel: United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction

videos.description:
